A scientist finds a metamorphic rock formation next to a magma chamber and determines that it was originally a sedimentary rock formation. He concludes that the temperature of the magma chamber caused the rock formation to become metamorphic. Which is another reliable explanation of how the rock formation was changed?
facts.truth: pressure on the rock formation inside Earth
distractors: weight of heavy machinery moving over the rocks
a sandstorm that caused the rocks to be eroded
a quick burial of the rocks by tons of volcanic ash

Which of these events is an example of a change in climate?
facts.truth: The average amount of annual precipitation decreases.
distractors: Afternoon clouds form and block the sunlight.
A series of tornadoes occurs over a one-week period.
An overnight temperature drop causes frost to form.

El Niño is a term that refers to the oceanic phenomenon in which the surface water temperatures increase. The increase in water temperature usually occurs in the tropical Pacific Ocean. Which will most likely occur as a result of this phenomenon to cause environmental changes?
distractors: melting of the polar ice caps
an increase in the length of summers

facts.context: The rover Spirit is a robotic probe that NASA has placed on Mars. The gravitational attraction of Mars is approximately 62% less than that of Earth. Compared to its measurements on Earth, on Mars the probe has
facts.truth: a smaller weight and the same mass.
distractors: the same weight and the same mass.
a larger weight, but smaller mass.
a smaller mass and larger weight.

facts.context: Earth is tilted as it travels around the Sun. The tilt changes the amount of energy each area gets from the Sun, which makes some months of the year warmer than others. What are the four main temperature changes in a year called?
Which is a fact rather than an opinion about earthquakes?
distractors: Earthquakes are bad for all local businesses.
Earthquakes always cause major damage to homes.

facts.context: Scientists theorize that the original atmosphere of Earth was stripped away by the solar wind shortly after the formation of the planet. The Jovian planets are far enough from the Sun that the solar wind has less of an impact. Studies of the Jovian planets suggest the early atmosphere of Earth was mainly composed of which two gases?
What covers most of the Earth's surface?
What causes the tectonic plates of Earth to continually move?
distractors: volcanic and seismic activity in the crust

facts.context: Structures in the universe are grouped into large systems, each of which are made up of smaller systems. Which of the following is the largest system of which Earth is only a very small part?

facts.truth: transferring heat to and from various regions.
distractors: spreading plant and animal life over a wider area.
ensuring ocean density is not the same globally.
carrying sediment from rivers and inland basins.

Which process moves water from the oceans to the atmosphere?
Which statement best compares gravity on the Moon to gravity on Earth?
facts.truth: Gravity on the Moon is less because it is smaller than the Earth.
distractors: Gravity on the Moon is greater because it is closer to the Sun.
Gravity on the Moon is less because it has less atmosphere than the Earth.
Gravity on the Moon is greater because it has less mass than the Earth.
